The single most impactful weakness is the CTA-to-affiliate mismatch: the mechanism builds anxiety around a tax-efficiency problem (ISA allowance loss), but the move lands on eToro — a trading platform — with zero bridging logic explaining why a retail CFD broker solves an ISA deadline problem. The affiliate lands as a naked product drop — "What financially-aware people use is Fidelity" is a sales line wearing a recommendation costume, and a 2am scroller's fraud radar fires the instant the sentence resolves. The mechanism earns genuine attention by explaining HMRC permanence, then the move wastes that trust by naming the product without a bridging proof layer. Next slot: insert one concrete specificity before the CTA — a fee figure, a setup time, or a single functional differentiator — so the affiliate arrives as evidence, not conclusion.

The affiliate CTA is the weakest link — "what financially-aware people use to navigate this is Fidelity Stocks & Shares ISA" is a limp, passive construction that collapses the urgency built by the mechanism into a soft product mention, breaking the chain between the permanent-loss framing and the action. The viewer feels sold at, not guided. Next slot: rewrite the CTA as a direct imperative tied to the deadline — "Open your Fidelity Stocks & Shares ISA before April 5" — active, deadline-anchored, no hedging.
